slide.title: Nature Portfolio
slide.notes: Welcome everyone. Today I'm showcasing five landscape shots from the Alpine region, taken during a two-week trek in July 2026. All photos shot on a Canon R5 with the RF 24-70mm f/2.8.
slide.subtitle: Alpine Landscapes — July 2026

slide.title: The Route
slide.notes: These five locations span 120km of trail. Start with the iconic Matterhorn reflection in Riffelsee at dawn, then the Aletsch glacier panorama from Eggishorn, the Engadin forest near St. Moritz, an alpine meadow at 2200m in Zermatt, and finish with the Ligurian coast near Portofino. Each slide has one photo — use the arrow keys to advance.
slide.text: Forest — Engadin pine forest, St. Moritz (1800m)

slide.title: Q3 Portfolio Results
slide.notes: Q3 was our strongest quarter. Print revenue rose every month, finishing at 92 thousand euros in September. On the right, direct referrals and social together account for nearly three quarters of new clients. The point is not just growth; it is increasingly diversified, repeatable growth.

slide.title: Momentum Is Compounding
slide.notes: Portfolio reach has more than doubled since April, from 18 thousand to 49 thousand monthly viewers. The acceleration begins after the Alpine series launches in July. Use the slope of the final three points to underline that the work is finding a larger audience each month.

slide.title: Signals at a Glance
slide.notes: Custom bullet icons are useful when a slide needs fast visual anchors rather than another chart. We crossed one hundred print orders, reached twelve thousand newsletter readers, and won the Alpine Photo Award. The final source line deliberately has no marker.
slide.text: 100+ print orders — the Alpine collection launched ahead of plan
12,400 readers — newsletter reach grew 38% quarter over quarter
Alpine Photo Award — landscape series selected as the jury winner

slide.title: Mountain — Matterhorn at Dawn
slide.notes: Shot at 6:15 AM — golden hour. The water was perfectly still for about 10 minutes before the wind picked up. ISO 100, f/8, 1/60s on a tripod. The reflection is razor-sharp because there was zero wind. This is the most-printed photo from the trip.

image.alt: Matterhorn reflected in Riffelsee at sunrise

slide.text: The Matterhorn mirrored in Riffelsee — 6:15 AM, zero wind, perfect reflection

slide.title: Ice and Forest
slide.notes: Left: the Aletsch glacier is the largest in the Alps at 23km long. Shot from Eggishorn at 11 AM with polarizing filter to cut glare. Right: Engadin pine forest at 5 PM with low-angle sun creating long shadows. Two completely different moods — glacial ice vs warm forest floor.

slide.text: Aletsch Glacier — 23km, largest in the Alps
Engadin Forest — old-growth stone pines at 1800m

slide.title: From Alps to Sea
slide.notes: Three perspectives from the last day of the trek. Left: alpine meadow at 2200m with blooming wildflowers in late July. Center: the Ligurian coast near Portofino at golden hour. Right: back to the Matterhorn at sunset with alpenglow — the mountain turns pink for about 8 minutes.

slide.title: C++ — Syntax Highlighting
slide.notes: Presenter now supports syntax-highlighted code blocks with lang= attribute. The monospace font and color palette make code readable on slides.

slide.title: Python — Multiline Block
slide.notes: Code blocks scale automatically: more lines = smaller rendering, fewer lines = larger.
slide.code:
def process_images(paths, size=(800, 600)): """Resize and convert a batch of images.
Uses bilinear interpolation for high-quality downscaling in multiple steps. """ results = [] for p in paths: img = load_image(p) if img is None: continue img = resize(img, size) results.append(img) return results

slide.title: Inline Code
slide.notes: Inline code uses monospace font and different color. Single-line expressions stay inline.
slide.text: Use std::vector<int> for dynamic arrays in C++
Rust uses let mut for mutable bindings

slide.title: Multiple Languages
slide.notes: Four different languages on one slide. The lang= attribute controls which keyword set is loaded.

slide.title: JSON & XML
slide.code: Demo notes A bullet point int x = 42;
